response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced staff/senior engineer with roughly 15+ years across web, embedded, and backend systems, which makes him significantly overqualified for an entry-level junior position. His Java exposure is peripheral (listed as a skill and used briefly at Altom), with his core expertise in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Critically, the role requires strong verbal and written German, while he reports only basic German. Seniority mismatch, compensation expectations, and the language gap make this a poor fit despite strong technical ability.
strengths: Deep software engineering experience with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Solid OOP foundations and completed computer science degree
Proven agile team experience and cloud/containerization exposure (AWS, Docker, Kubernetes)
concerns: Only basic German, while the role requires strong German communication
Severely overqualified for an entry-level junior role — retention and salary fit risk
Java is not a primary language; main stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and agile experience
